_note: >>> GPU TESTING PATCH (Option B) <<< — H100×2 self-hosted deploy spec. Provides the deploy step that rag-enable-vlm and rag-enable-guardrails (downstream h100 skills) need a running RAG stack against. Trigger via ci/run_skill_eval_h100.sh. The adapter (skill-eval/adapters/rag-blueprint/generate.py) ignores unknown top-level fields like _note, so this is purely a marker for humans reading the file.

H100_x2.description: 2x H100 80GB PCIe per docs/support-matrix.md — self-hosted Docker Compose default. Required so rag-enable-vlm and rag-enable-guardrails (downstream h100 skills) have a deployed RAG stack to run against.

env: Linux host with 2x NVIDIA H100 80GB GPUs, driver 560+, nvidia-container-toolkit installed, Docker + Docker Compose plugin running. Self-hosted deployment — all NIM containers (LLM + embedding + ranking + ingestion) run locally on the H100s using deploy/compose/.env (NOT nvdev.env). Required env var: NGC_API_KEY (nvapi-) for nvcr.io pulls. cwd: ${RAG_REPO_ROOT}/. Bring up the vector DB (vectordb.yaml), local NIMs (nims.yaml), ingestor server, and rag server. All model inference must use the local NIM containers — do NOT use cloud endpoints (integrate.api.nvidia.com).

expects.query: Use the rag-deploy-blueprint skill to deploy NVIDIA RAG Blueprint in self-hosted Docker Compose mode on the local H100 GPUs. Source deploy/compose/.env, then start the vector DB (vectordb.yaml), local NIMs (nims.yaml), ingestor server (docker-compose-ingestor-server.yaml), and rag server (docker-compose-rag-server.yaml). All inference must use the local NIM containers — not cloud endpoints. The eval runs from inside the repository checkout; use git rev-parse --show-toplevel if you need the absolute path.
checks: The agent's trajectory shows it read the rag-deploy-blueprint SKILL.md before any deployment commands

The agent's trajectory shows it sourced deploy/compose/.env (NOT nvdev.env) before running docker compose up — verifiable via a `source` of .env or `--env-file deploy/compose/.env` in a docker compose invocation

`docker ps --format '{{.Names}}' | grep -E '^(nim-llm-ms|nemo(tron|retriever)-(embedding|ranking)-ms)$' | wc -l` outputs a number greater than or equal to 3 (the canonical local-NIM trio per deploy/compose/nims.yaml; the regex accepts both nemotron-* and nemoretriever-* prefix variants in case of future renames)

`nvidia-smi --query-gpu=memory.used --format=csv,noheader,nounits | awk '{s+=$1} END{print s}'` outputs a number greater than 5000 (NIMs have loaded weights into GPU memory, >5 GB total across the 2 H100s)

expects.query: Verify the self-hosted RAG stack is healthy and the local NIMs are reachable. Hit the rag-server and ingestor-server health endpoints, and confirm the local NIM containers are Up. Report per-service status.
checks: `docker exec rag-server env 2>/dev/null | grep -E '^APP_LLM_SERVERURL='` outputs a line that does NOT contain integrate.api.nvidia.com (must point at a local NIM endpoint, never the cloud endpoint)

`docker ps --format '{{.Names}}\t{{.Status}}' | grep -E '^(nim-llm-ms|nemo(tron|retriever)-(embedding|ranking)-ms)\b' | grep -v 'Up' | wc -l` outputs 0 (every running local-NIM container is in 'Up' state — none Restarting / Created / Exited)

The agent's final response reports a per-service health verdict for rag-server, ingestor-server, and at least one local NIM (nim-llm-ms or nemotron-*-ms / nemoretriever-*-ms), each with a clear status indicator (HTTP 200, 'Healthy', 'Up', or equivalent). A single overall summary alone is NOT sufficient — per-service breakdown is required
